configurations.name: DD_AGENT_HOST
configurations.description: The host name to use to connect the Datadog agent for traces. The host name can be IPv4, IPv6, or a domain name. If DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL is specified, the value of DD_AGENT_HOST is ignored.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether API Security features are enabled. If unset, API Security is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_API_SECURITY_SAMPLE_DELAY
configurations.description: Sets the delay (in seconds) between API Security schema samples. This setting is intended for system testing and is not meant for general use.

configurations.name: DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables sending APM trace data. When disabled, tracing still runs to support other products, but only periodic heartbeat traces and manually kept traces are sent.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTOMATED_USER_EVENTS_TRACKING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables automatic emission of AppSec user lifecycle signals (login success/failure, signup, authenticated user) into span metadata. Use when you want account-abuse and auth-related detections without manually emitting SDK events.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_AUTO_USER_INSTRUMENTATION_MODE
configurations.description: Specifies which collection mode to use for automated user events.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_CLI_START_ON_RINIT
configurations.description: Forces AppSec request lifecycle startup during PHP RINIT for CLI-like runtimes (notably special worker/server models). Use only when your runtime does not match the default listener-based lifecycle behavior and AppSec initialization timing needs to be changed.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the AppSec product inside the tracer. When enabled the tracer initializes the AppSec module (in-app WAF, header/body collection and AppSec telemetry)

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_EXTRA_HEADERS
configurations.description: Adds additional HTTP header names to be collected and analyzed by AppSec beyond the built-in relevant header set. Use when your environment carries security-relevant context in custom headers (gateway IDs, tenant IDs, forwarded auth context, etc.).

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HELPER_LOG_FILE
configurations.description: Controls where the external AppSec helper writes its own logs. Use for low-level troubleshooting of helper startup/IPC issues.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HELPER_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Sets verbosity for helper process logs. Increase only during debugging to investigate helper connection/initialization problems.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HELPER_PATH
configurations.description: Filesystem path to the AppSec helper binary/library used by the extension. Use only for custom packaging/layouts or debugging non-standard installations.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HELPER_RUNTIME_PATH
configurations.description: Base directory used to derive helper runtime artifacts (socket/lock paths) and related telemetry tags. Use when default runtime paths are unsuitable for your container/runtime filesystem constraints.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HELPER_RUST_REDIRECTION
configurations.description: Whether to look for the new rust appsec helper library next to the configured helper location and load that instead.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_HTML
configurations.description: Path to a local HTML template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be HTML. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_HTTP_BLOCKED_TEMPLATE_JSON
configurations.description: Path to a local JSON template file that will be returned when a request is blocked and the response should be JSON. If unset or invalid, the tracer uses the default blocking HTML template defined by the product RFC. The tracer decides HTML or JSON according to `Accept` header or the block action's parameters.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_LOG_FILE
configurations.description: Selects destination for AppSec extension logs (php_error_reporting, syslog, stdout, stderr, or file path).

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: Controls AppSec extension log verbosity (off, fatal, error, warning/warn, info, debug, trace).

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_BODY_BUFF_SIZE
configurations.description: Limits how many bytes of request/response body are buffered for AppSec inspection. Increase when detections require larger payload visibility; decrease to reduce memory/copy overhead.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACES
configurations.description: Limits how many separate stacktraces are captured and attached to an AppSec/IAST event to keep payload sizes reasonable. Evidence stacktraces are useful for triage, but unbounded capture can leak sensitive data and consume excessive bandwidth; this limit balances evidence value and safety. Set to 0 to collect all.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_MAX_STACK_TRACE_DEPTH
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of stack frames retained for any captured stacktrace attached to AppSec events. Tracers should prioritise top-of-stack frames and truncate deeper frames when this limit is reached. The setting helps keep evidentiary stacktraces compact while preserving the most actionable frames

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_KEY_REGEXP
configurations.description: A regular expression used by the AppSec obfuscator to identify parameter keys that must cause full redaction. When any `key_path` string matches this regexp the entire parameter is replaced by `<redacted by datadog>`. The default key regexp matches common secret/key names (password, token, authorization, jwt, etc.) an empty string disables the key regexp

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_OBFUSCATION_PARAMETER_VALUE_REGEXP
configurations.description: A regex string to redact sensitive data by its value in attack reports.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RASP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Turns on the tracer's RASP / Exploit Prevention capabilities. When enabled the tracer runs synchronous, pre-execution checks and may generate block actions or exploit signals. RASP may have separate activation and implementation details per language; enabling it usually activates extra synchronous instrumentation and additional evidence capture for detections

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_RULES
configurations.description: Override the default rules file provided. Must be a path to a valid JSON rules file

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_SCA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables the tracer's runtime Software Composition Analysis (Runtime SCA) capability, which reports runtime dependency/SBOM information and powers runtime vulnerability detection. Useful for testing and demonstrations as well as production runtime vulnerability workflows; SCA may have billing/usage implications and language compatibility constraints described in the SCA runbooks

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_STACK_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables security-related stack traces generation when security events occur.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TESTING
configurations.description: Enables AppSec test-mode code paths and testing helper functions not intended for production behavior.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TESTING_ABORT_RINIT
configurations.description: In test mode, forces abort/block behavior during request init to exercise blocking paths in tests.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TESTING_HELPER_METRICS
configurations.description: In test mode, controls emission of helper connection telemetry metrics to avoid test noise/flakiness.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TESTING_RAW_BODY
configurations.description: In test mode, sends raw request body payloads for test assertions and protocol verification.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Controls the maximum amount of AppSec traces, per second.

configurations.name: DD_APPSEC_WAF_TIMEOUT
configurations.description: Limits the WAF synchronous execution time (in microseconds).

configurations.name: DD_AUTOFINISH_SPANS
configurations.description: Whether spans are automatically finished when the tracer is flushed.

configurations.name: DD_CODE_ORIGIN_FOR_SPANS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables code origin for spans behavior in the tracer. When disabled, related data collection and processing are skipped.

configurations.name: DD_CODE_ORIGIN_MAX_USER_FRAMES
configurations.description: Code Origin for spans (Dynamic Instrumentation): sets the maximum number of user stack frames to capture/consider when recording code origin (default: 8). Note: the current code-origin recorder reads this value but does not use it, so it may have no effect in this version.

configurations.name: DD_CRASHTRACKING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Crash Tracking. When enabled, the tracer initializes crash tracking and configures JVM crash/OOM hooks (for example, `-XX:OnError` and `-XX:OnOutOfMemoryError`) to run Datadog-provided scripts that upload `hs_err_pid*.log` crash logs and OOME notifications. Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_INJECT_SQL_BASEHASH
configurations.description: Enables the injection of a "base hash" in SQL queries when DBM propagation is also enabled. This base hash is used to find matching spans, and enrich the queries with related values.

configurations.name: DD_DBM_PROPAGATION_MODE
configurations.description: Enables linking between data sent from APM and the Database Monitoring product when set to `service` or `full`. The `service` option enables the connection between DBM and APM services. The `full` option enables connection between database spans with database query events. Available for Postgres and MySQL.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST
configurations.description: Override the address of the trace Agent host that the default tracer attempts to submit DogStatsD metrics to. Use `DD_AGENT_HOST` to override `DD_DOGSTATSD_HOST`.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the DogStatsD Agent that metrics are submitted to. If the Agent configuration sets `dogstatsd_port` or `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` to something other than the default `8125`, then this tracing library `DD_DOGSTATSD_PORT` must match it.

configurations.name: DD_DOGSTATSD_URL
configurations.description: The URL to use to connect the Datadog agent for Dogstatsd metrics.

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_IDENTIFIERS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of additional identifier keywords to redact in captured snapshot data

configurations.name: DD_DYNAMIC_INSTRUMENTATION_REDACTED_TYPES
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of object types to redact from dynamic logs and snapshots. Supports * wildcards (e.g. Secret* matches SecretKey).

configurations.name: DD_ENV
configurations.description: Sets the global `env` for all data emitted by the Agent. If `env` is not present in your trace data, this variable is used.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_CAPTURE_INTERVAL_SECONDS
configurations.description: Exception Replay: minimum interval in seconds between capturing the same exception fingerprint again. Used to rate-limit repeated captures of identical exceptions. Default: 3600 seconds.

configurations.name: DD_EXCEPTION_REPLAY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Exception Replay (Live Debugging): enables the Exception Replay product. When enabled (for example via Remote Config), the tracer starts/stops the Exception Replay subsystem. This key is also accepted as a backward-compatible alias for `DD_EXCEPTION_DEBUGGING_ENABLED`.

configurations.name: DD_EXPERIMENTAL_PROPAGATE_PROCESS_TAGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether process-level tags (such as entrypoint metadata) are collected and included in tracing metadata. If unset, this is enabled by default.

configurations.name: DD_HTTP_SERVER_ROUTE_BASED_NAMING
configurations.description: When set to `false` http framework routes are not used for resource names. _This can change resource names and derived metrics if changed._

configurations.name: DD_INJECT_FORCE
configurations.description: Single Step Instrumentation (SSI): forces the tracer javaagent to load even when multiple JVM agents are detected. This bypasses SSI guardrails that would otherwise abort startup to avoid running multiple agents. Can be set via `DD_INJECT_FORCE` or system property `dd.inject.force`. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_INSTRUMENTATION_TELEMETRY_ENABLED
configurations.description: Datadog may collect environmental and diagnostic information about your system to improve the product. When false, this telemetry data will not be collected. **Default**: true

configurations.name: DD_METRICS_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables OpenTelemetry metrics export. Metrics are disabled by default and are only enabled when this is set to a truthy value.

configurations.name: DD_OPENAI_LOGS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable collection of prompts and completions as logs. You can adjust the rate of prompts and completions collected using the sample rate configuration described below.

configurations.name: DD_OPENAI_LOG_PROMPT_COMPLETION_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Configure the sample rate for the collection of prompts and completions as logs.

configurations.name: DD_OPENAI_SERVICE
configurations.description: The service name reported by default for OpenAI requests.

configurations.name: DD_OPENAI_SPAN_CHAR_LIMIT
configurations.description: Configure the maximum number of characters for the following data within span tags:

configurations.name: DD_OPENAI_SPAN_PROMPT_COMPLETION_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Configure the sample rate for the collection of prompts and completions as span tags.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ALLOCATION_SAMPLING_DISTANCE
configurations.description: Average number of allocated bytes between allocation samples. Lower values improve allocation hotspot precision but increase profiler overhead.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_ENDPOINT_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether spans add a pprof label with the top-level request endpoint so profiles can be grouped by endpoint. Defaults to true.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXCEPTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables exception-event profiling so exception-heavy code paths appear in profiles. Useful when diagnosing error storms or control-flow via exceptions.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXCEPTION_MESSAGE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Includes exception message text in collected exception samples. Enable only when message content is needed for debugging and acceptable under your data-sensitivity policy.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXCEPTION_SAMPLING_DISTANCE
configurations.description: Sampling interval for exception profiling events. Lower values capture more exception detail; higher values reduce overhead.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_CPU_TIME_ENABLED
configurations.description: Collects CPU-time sample dimension in addition to wall-time. Use when you need to separate true CPU saturation from waiting/blocking behavior.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_FEATURES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Umbrella switch that enables experimental profiling capabilities. Intended for evaluation/early adoption; validate overhead and correctness before broad rollout.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_IO_ENABLED
configurations.description: Instruments low-level file/socket I/O operations so profiles can attribute time to read/write activity (not just CPU). Use when investigating latency dominated by disk/network waits or unknown blocking in external calls.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_OUTPUT_PPROF
configurations.description: Writes profiles to local files instead of uploading.

configurations.name: DD_PROFILING_TIMELINE_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable the timeline profile type. Added in version `0.89.0`. **Note**: This supersedes the `DD_PROFILING_EXPERIMENTAL_TIMELINE_ENABLED` environment variable (`datadog.profiling.experimental_timeline_enabled` INI setting), which was available since `0.89` (default `0`). If both are set, this one takes precedence.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Remote Config polling in the tracer (fetches configuration updates from the remote config endpoint and applies them to subscribed products). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_REMOTE_CONFIG_POLL_INTERVAL_SECONDS
configurations.description: **Since**: 0.2.0 Sets how often, in seconds, the Datadog Agent is queried for Remote Configuration updates.

configurations.name: DD_SERVICE_MAPPING
configurations.description: Provide service names for each plugin. Accepts comma separated plugin:service-name pairs, with or without spaces.

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Span sampling rules to keep individual spans when the rest of the trace would otherwise be dropped. A JSON array of objects. Rules are applied in configured order to determine the span's sample rate. The sample_rate value must be between 0.0 and 1.0 (inclusive). For more information, see Ingestion Mechanisms. Example: - Set the span sample rate to 50% for the service my-service and operation name http.request, up to 50 traces per second: '[{"service": "my-service", "name": "http.request", "sample_rate":0.5, "max_per_second": 50}]'

configurations.name: DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES_FILE
configurations.description: Points to a JSON file that contains the span sampling rules. DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES takes precedence over this variable. See DD_SPAN_SAMPLING_RULES for the rule format.

configurations.name: DD_TAGS
configurations.description: Custom tags applied to traces profiles and metrics, provided as comma-separated key:value pairs (e.g. `layer:api,team:intake`).

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL
configurations.description: Sets the interval for the telemetry status heartbeat (in seconds)

configurations.name: DD_TELEMETRY_LOG_COLLECTION_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Default**: true : Enable or disable log collection for telemetry.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_GENERATION_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether new traces use 128-bit W3C trace IDs (32-character hexadecimal strings) or 64-bit Datadog trace IDs (16-character hexadecimal strings). The default is true to support W3C trace context propagation.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_128_BIT_TRACEID_LOGGING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether 128-bit trace IDs are logged in their full 32-character format or truncated to 16 characters. Set to false for compatibility with systems that expect the shorter format.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT
configurations.description: The port of the Trace Agent that the tracer submits to. If the Agent configuration sets receiver_port or DD_APM_RECEIVER_PORT to something other than the default 8126, then DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT or DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL must match it.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AGENT_URL
configurations.description: Sets the URL endpoint where profiles are sent. Overrides `DD_AGENT_HOST` and `DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT` if set. Defaults to `http://<DD_AGENT_HOST>:<DD_TRACE_AGENT_PORT>`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AMQP_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables trace analytics for spans produced by RabbitMQ AMQP instrumentation (publish/consume and Channel command spans)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AMQP_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by RabbitMQ AMQP spans

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_AMQP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables RabbitMQ AMQP client instrumentation (creates spans for Channel commands and message publish/consume, and propagates trace context via AMQP headers)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ANALYTICS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables Trace Analytics by default. When enabled for an integration, instrumentations set the `analytics.sample_rate` metric on spans (using the configured per-integration sample rate). Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BAGGAGE_MAX_BYTES
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the maximum number of bytes that can be injected into the baggage header when propagating to a downstream service. Default value is 8192 bytes.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BAGGAGE_MAX_ITEMS
configurations.description: Configuration key to set the maximum number of items that can be injected into the baggage header when propagating to a downstream service. Default value is 64 items.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_BAGGAGE_TAG_KEYS
configurations.description: Comma-separated list of baggage keys to copy into span tags. Set to * to tag all baggage items; if unset, a default allowlist is used.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables or disables the automatic collection of client IP addresses from HTTP request headers. When enabled, the IP address is stored in the http.client_ip span tag.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER
configurations.description: Configures a custom header name from which to source the http.client_ip tag value. If this variable is set, all other IP-related headers are ignored (for example, setting DD_TRACE_CLIENT_IP_HEADER=custom-ip-header and including the header custom-ip-header: 5.6.7.9 in a request results in a span tagged with "http.client_ip": "5.6.7.9"). If an empty string or null value is passed, IP headers are queried in this order: - x-forwarded-for - x-real-ip - true-client-ip - x-client-ip - x-forwarded - forwarded-for - x-cluster-client-ip - fastly-client-ip - cf-connecting-ip - cf-connecting-ipv6

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_CLI_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable tracing of PHP scripts from the CLI. See Tracing CLI scripts.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DB_CLIENT_SPLIT_BY_INSTANCE
configurations.description: Set the service name of HTTP requests to `pdo- `. For example, a `PDO->query()` call to a database host `datadoghq.com` has the service name `pdo-datadoghq.com` instead of the default service name of `pdo`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_DEBUG
configurations.description: Enable debug logging in the tracer. Use OTEL_LOG_LEVEL as an alias

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ELASTICSEARCH_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Elasticsearch integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ELASTICSEARCH_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for elasticsearch. When disabled, spans for elasticsearch operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Default**: true Enable web framework and library instrumentation. When false, the application code doesn't generate any traces. See also DD_APM_TRACING_ENABLED.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_FORKED_PROCESS
configurations.description: If the application invokes `pcntl_unshare(CLONE_NEWUSER);` and the tracer is installed, the application fatally crashes. This happens because `unshare` with `CLONE_NEWUSER` requires the process not to be threaded, while the PHP tracer uses a separate thread to send traces to the Datadog Agent without blocking the main process.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_GIT_METADATA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Git metadata: when enabled, adds git repository URL and commit SHA tags to the root span (used for linking traces to source code). Default: true.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HEADER_TAGS
configurations.description: Automatically apply specified HTTP headers as span tags. If a custom tag name is not specified, the tag key defaults to http.request.headers.<normalized-header-name> for request headers and http.response.headers.<normalized-header-name> for response headers.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_CLIENT_SPLIT_BY_DOMAIN
configurations.description: Set the service name of HTTP requests to `host- `, for example a `curl_exec()` call to `https://datadoghq.com` has the service name `host-datadoghq.com` instead of the default service name of `curl`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_HTTP_URL_QUERY_PARAM_ALLOWED
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of query parameters to be collected as part of the URL. Set to empty to prevent collecting any parameters, or `*` to collect all parameters. Added in version `0.74.0`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_INFERRED_PROXY_SERVICES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Inferred proxy services: when enabled, registers the inferred-proxy propagator to extract `x-dd-proxy*` headers and start an inferred proxy span (currently supports `x-dd-proxy=aws-apigateway`) as a parent of the server span, using header values for service/resource/tags. Default: false.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_KAFKA_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the Kafka integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_KAFKA_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the Kafka integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LARAVEL_QUEUE_DISTRIBUTED_TRACING
configurations.description: Disables the creation of an additional `laravel.queue.process` span and relies solely on span links. Added in version `0.93.0`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LOG_FILE
configurations.description: Specifies a log file. If none is specified, logs go to the default PHP error location. To debug datadog-ipc-helper issues (for example, submission of telemetry), you must specify the log file.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_LOG_LEVEL
configurations.description: A string for the minimum log level for the tracer to use when debug logging is enabled, for example, error, debug.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MEASURE_COMPILE_TIME
configurations.description: Record the compile time of the request (in milliseconds) onto the top-level span.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MEMCACHED_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for memcached. When disabled, spans for memcached operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MONGODB_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for mongodb. When disabled, spans for mongodb operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MONGO_ANALYTICS_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Sampling rate for analytics events generated by the MongoDB integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_MONGO_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing for the MongoDB integration.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_OBFUSCATION_QUERY_STRING_REGEXP
configurations.description: Applies a regex to redact sensitive data from query strings on incoming HTTP requests. The default regex matches various sensitive data patterns, including passwords, tokens, API keys, private keys, and authorization terms. Matches are replaced with <redacted>. If an empty string is passed, no obfuscation occurs. The resulting value is reported in the http.url tag.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_OPENAI_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables instrumentation for openai. When disabled, spans for openai operations are not created.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_OTEL_ENABLED
configurations.description: **Description**: Enables the Datadog SDK's OpenTelemetry interoperability for traces. **Notes**: The default is `true` in the Java SDK.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PEER_SERVICE_DEFAULTS_ENABLED
configurations.description: Peer service defaults (Naming Schema v0): when enabled, computes default `peer.service` values for eligible spans using the v1 peer-service default algorithm; when disabled (default), Naming Schema v0 does not compute peer.service defaults.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PEER_SERVICE_MAPPING
configurations.description: Maps detected peer service names to normalized values before they are attached to spans.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats from which to attempt to inject and extract distributed tracing propagation data. The first format found with complete and valid headers is used to define the trace to continue. The more specific DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT and DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_EXTRACT configurations take priority when present.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_EXTRACT
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats from which to attempt to extract distributed tracing propagation data. The first format found with complete and valid headers is used to define the trace to continue.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_PROPAGATION_STYLE_INJECT
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of header formats to include to propagate distributed traces between services.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RATE_LIMIT
configurations.description: Sets the maximum number of traces to sample per second; applies only when either DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES or DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE is set.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REDIS_CLIENT_SPLIT_BY_HOST
configurations.description: Set the service name of Redis clients operations to `redis- `.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REMOVE_AUTOINSTRUMENTATION_ORPHANS
configurations.description: Automatically remove orphaned spans generated by auto-instrumentation. Currently, this only applies to some Redis and Laravel calls used in the context of Laravel Horizon. Added in version `0.88.0`. **Note:** These orphaned spans are flushed but not recorded in the trace. Moreover, the specific single-span traces that are removed with this configuration option are: - `laravel.event.handle`

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REMOVE_INTEGRATION_SERVICE_NAMES_ENABLED
configurations.description: When enabled, disables inferred-services/integration service naming by making Naming Schema v0 not allow inferred services (so integrations that rely on inferred services fall back to application service naming)

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_REPORT_HOSTNAME
configurations.description: Whether to report the system's hostname for each trace. When disabled, the hostname of the Agent is used instead.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_RENAMING_ALWAYS_SIMPLIFIED_ENDPOINT
configurations.description: Always set the http.endpoint tag with the results of the simplification algorithm of Resource Renaming

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_RENAMING_ENABLED
configurations.description: Controls whether the http.endpoint tag is computed and added for incoming HTTP requests. This is disabled by default unless application security is enabled at startup, and can be explicitly enabled or disabled with this setting.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_URI_FRAGMENT_REGEX
configurations.description: CSV of regexes that identifies path fragments corresponding to IDs (see Map resource names to normalized URI).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_URI_MAPPING_INCOMING
configurations.description: CSV of URI mappings to normalize resource naming for incoming requests (see Map resource names to normalized URI).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_URI_MAPPING_OUTGOING
configurations.description: CSV of URI mappings to normalize resource naming for outgoing requests (see Map resource names to normalized URI).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RESOURCE_URI_QUERY_PARAM_ALLOWED
configurations.description: A comma-separated list of query parameters to be collected as part of the resource URI. Set to empty to prevent collecting any parameters, or `*` to collect all parameters. Added in version `0.74.0`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_RETAIN_THREAD_CAPABILITIES
configurations.description: Works for Linux. Set to `true` to retain capabilities on Datadog background threads when you change the effective user ID. This option does not affect most setups, but some modules - to date Datadog is only aware of Apache's mod-ruid2 - may invoke `setuid()` or similar syscalls, leading to crashes or loss of functionality as it loses capabilities. **Note:** Enabling this option may compromise security. This option, standalone, does not pose a security risk. However, an attacker being able to exploit a vulnerability in PHP or web server may be able to escalate privileges with relative ease, if the web server or PHP were started with full capabilities, as the background threads will retain their original capabilities. Datadog recommends restricting the capabilities of the web server with the `setcap` utility.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLE_RATE
configurations.description: Controls the ingestion sample rate (between 0.0 and 1.0) between the Agent and the backend.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES
configurations.description: Configures custom sampling rules for traces. Rules are evaluated in order, and the first matching rule determines the sampling rate. If no rules match, the default sampling rate is used. For more information about how these configurations affect trace ingestion, see Ingestion Mechanisms].

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SAMPLING_RULES_FORMAT
configurations.description: Sets the format of custom sampling rules. Valid values are regex or glob. If the value is not recognized, trace sampling rules are disabled.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SIDECAR_BACKPRESSURE_BYTES
configurations.description: Maximum buffered bytes before the data on the sidecar channel starts being dropped.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SIDECAR_BACKPRESSURE_QUEUE
configurations.description: Maximum amount of unprocessed messages the sidecar may receive before messages are being dropped.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SIDECAR_CONNECTION_MODE
configurations.description:
Determines the sidecar connection mode:
- subprocess: Creates a subprocess accessible by many other processes running under the same user.
- thread: Runs the sidecar in threaded mode, only accessible from forks.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SPANS_LIMIT
configurations.description: The maximum number of spans that are generated within one trace. If the maximum number of spans is reached, then spans are no longer generated. If the limit is increased, then the amount of memory that is used by a pending trace will increase and might reach the PHP maximum amount of allowed memory. The maximum amount of allowed memory can be increased with the PHP INI system setting `memory_limit`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_STARTUP_LOGS
configurations.description: Enable startup configuration and diagnostic log.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SYMFONY_MESSENGER_DISTRIBUTED_TRACING
configurations.description: When disabled, causal produce/consume relationships will be associated using span links. Added in version `1.3.0`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_SYMFONY_MESSENGER_MIDDLEWARES
configurations.description: Enables the tracing of Symfony Messenger middlewares. Added in version `1.3.0`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_URL_AS_RESOURCE_NAMES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enable URL's as resource names (see Map resource names to normalized URI).

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_ENABLED
configurations.description: Enables tracing sent and received websocket messages (text and binary) and connection close events.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_INHERIT_SAMPLING
configurations.description: By default, websocket messages preserve the same sampling as the span captured during the handshake. This ensures that, if a handshake span has been sampled, all the messages in its session will also be sampled. To disable that behavior and sample each websocket message independently, set this configuration to `false`.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_WEBSOCKET_MESSAGES_SEPARATE_TRACES
configurations.description: By default, each received message generates a new trace. The handshake is linked to it as a span link. Setting this parameter to `false` causes all the spans captured during the session to be in the same trace.

configurations.name: DD_TRACE_X_DATADOG_TAGS_MAX_LENGTH
configurations.description: Maximum length of the `x-datadog-tags` header for trace tag propagation. Set to 0 to disable.

configurations.name: DD_VERSION
configurations.description: Set the application's version, for example: `1.2.3` or `6c44da20`.

configurations.name: OTEL_PROPAGATORS
configurations.description: Specifies trace context propagation formats for both extraction and injection (comma-separated list). Lowest precedence; ignored if any other Datadog trace context propagation environment variable is set.

configurations.name: OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES
configurations.description: Sets OpenTelemetry resource attributes that are mapped to tracer tags when the corresponding tracer tag setting is not set. Reserved attributes for service, environment, and version are mapped to standard tags, and only the first 10 attributes are applied.
